key_insight: A hybrid material point method can accurately reproduce bending of highly flexible slender structures by capturing the coupling between cross-sectional deformation and global curvature.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical simulation tool for predicting large-deformation behavior in tubes, tape springs, soft robots, and deployable structures under bending and contact.
method: Built a hybrid-MPM framework combining Lagrangian finite elements with Eulerian finite differences.
Simulated experimental three-point bending tests for elastic tubes and tape springs.
Compared numerical predictions with desktop experiments and classical bending theory.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports excellent agreement between simulations, experiments, and classical theory for canonical bending cases.
watch_item: Performance may depend on careful treatment of complex contact mechanics and calibration for specific slender-structure geometries.
paper_title: Numerical and experimental framework for bending elasticity of highly flexible slender structures
